scripts.dev: trap 'kill 0' EXIT; tsup --watch --no-dts & vite --config vite.docs.config.ts & wait

principles: Simplicity above all — the best solution is the simplest one that fully solves the problem.
Hold yourself to a staff engineer standard; only propose changes you would confidently ship to production.
Understand before modifying — read surrounding code and follow its conventions.
Build from small, composable pieces; let the type system carry its weight.
Formatting is tooling's job. Never fight the formatter.
Solve the stated problem — not adjacent ones. A bug fix is not a refactoring opportunity.
Extend before inventing — prefer growing an existing module over creating a new one.
Dependencies flow inward. Shared packages never depend on application code.
Abstractions are extracted, not predicted — duplication across multiple call sites earns a shared utility.
Never update a shared component without explicit approval; propose the change and let the user decide.
Imperative mood, atomic commits — each commit represents one logical change.
Feature branches for non-trivial work; never force-push shared branches.
Review your own diff before committing.

vocabularyGlossary.Manifest: the canonical project profile JSON at ./manifest.json that downstream skills consume
vocabularyGlossary.primitive: low-level UI building block under packages/ui/src/primitives
vocabularyGlossary.Slot: named region inside a composite component

mandatorySkills.trigger: asked to discover, profile, fingerprint, or summarize the project
asked to create, add, build, or scaffold a new UI component
asked to recommend or identify new UI components
asked to create or scaffold a docs page for a UI component
asked to audit the project's component docs / demos / stories
asked to audit, check, or review a UI component
asked to create or scaffold tests
asked to audit the project's tests
asked to wrap up a task or commit; a non-empty diff exists
asked to review changes before a commit
asked to audit TypeScript code for smells
asked to audit accessibility, or after a new frontend component
asked to deliberate, scope, or shape a fuzzy idea
asked to audit, review, or critique a decision with meaningful tradeoffs
a concrete plan exists and is about to execute
asked to compose or scaffold a new project skill
asked to audit or tighten a project skill
asked to recommend new project skills to add

notes: No CI provider detected — repository has no `.github/workflows/`, `.gitlab-ci.yml`, or `.circleci/config.yml`. `ci.provider` is null and `ci.jobs` is empty; the pre-commit hook (lefthook) is the only automated gate.

heimdall declares react and next only as devDependencies and peerDependencies with an empty `dependencies` object; classified as `library` because its `main`/`exports` point at compiled JS and it carries no runtime framework deps.
sindri declares react/next only as peer/dev deps but ships React components (e.g. login-page.tsx, sidebar-user-menu.tsx); classified as `react`.
Only `packages/ui` has a test runner; admin, chat, tms, heimdall, and sindri declare no `test` script.

Apps (admin, chat, tms) live entirely under `app/` (Next App Router) with no `components/`, `hooks/`, `primitives/`, or `tokens/` subdirectory matching the schema's search order; all four directory fields are null.
Only `CLAUDE.md` is present as a conventions doc — no `AGENTS.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, or `README.md` at the repo root.
